prose:
Fukui reactivity analysis for benzaldehyde

Method provenance
Property: Condensed Fukui functions f+, f–, f0 (average) and dual descriptor per atom; most electrophilic (max f+) and nucleophilic (max f–) sites highlighted.  
Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier "standard"), no density fitting (exact four-center integrals) in PySCF 2.x via chemkit.  
Geometry provenance: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates from /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/benzaldehyde/benzaldehyde.xyz; single-point charge analyses on this fixed geometry (no re-optimization).  
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation).  
Charge 0, multiplicity 1 (closed-shell singlet).  
SCF calculations for the N, N+1, and N–1 states all converged; Mulliken charges were used to build condensed Fukui indices.  
Convergence & integrity: run completed with integrity.status = "ok" and trustworthy = true.  
Live engine log was streamed to the standard chemkit .out file in the working directory during execution (path provided at job launch); result JSON written to /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmp1ri4idim/live.json.

Headline results (units dimensionless)
• Most electrophilic site: atom 3 (C) with f+ = 0.39938971  
• Most nucleophilic site: atom 1 (O) with f– = 0.23839906  
• Dual descriptor highlights atom 3 (Δf = +0.3626) and atom 1 (Δf = –0.1404) as the dominant electrophilic and nucleophilic centers, respectively.

The analysis indicates that the carbonyl carbon (atom 3) is the primary electrophilic center, while the carbonyl oxygen (atom 1) is the primary nucleophilic site under gas-phase B3LYP/def2-TZVP conditions.
